iterations.strategy: Root cause is a storage-level representation mismatch: runtime build_joins repeatedly scans all 15M orders and materializes a sparse orderkey->year array (cache-exceeding initialization plus per-row date extraction) even though Q9 only needs year lookup by orderkey during probes. Category E strategy: add a persistent dense year lookup extension keyed by o_orderkey and mmap it directly in execution; keep selective green-part filtering and lineitem composite-index probing unchanged, removing the recurring orders preprocessing work from hot path setup.

Root cause is storage-level and work-proportional mismatch: the hot path still re-evaluates varlen p_name LIKE '%green%' over all 2M part rows and then scans all 8M partsupp rows even though only a small qualifying subset contributes to joins. Category E with work elimination: add a persistent dense partkey->has_green lookup extension to replace runtime string scanning, then restructure partsupp filtering to keyed range retrieval on sorted ps_partkey so only qualifying ranges are materialized before probing lineitem_partsupp_hash; keep dense orderkey->year extension and direct-array aggregation unchanged.

Root cause is a cache-exceeding random-access pattern in main_scan: probing lineitem_partsupp_hash from filtered partsupp rows drives scattered lineitem row fetches across large columns, causing latency-bound misses and poor locality. Category B (algorithm change) with plan restructuring: switch to a lineitem-driven sequential scan, apply green-part filtering via dense has_green lookup, do point lookups into existing partsupp_pk_hash for supplycost, and keep dense o_orderkey->year plus direct-array nation/year aggregation so the dominant work becomes bandwidth-friendly sequential scanning instead of random fact-row access.

Root cause is a combined Category B/C mismatch: iter2 touches a selective subset but reads lineitem measures through random rowid access and repeatedly executes expensive floating conversion/rounding per matched row, making main_scan latency and compute heavy. The revised plan keeps selective green-part and partsupp pruning, but materializes matched lineitem rowids with nation and scaled supplycost payload, sorts by rowid to turn lineitem measure access into locality-friendly sequential reads, and uses existing scaled int16 discount/quantity column versions to remove per-row floating conversion in the hot loop while preserving direct-array nation/year aggregation.

Root cause is Category C work-proportional waste inside main_scan: iter_2 still performs expensive per-match floating conversions/rounding for l_discount and l_quantity (and repeatedly redoes derivable scaling work), inflating CPU time even after selective pruning. The revised plan keeps the fast selective partsupp-driven join shape from iter_2 but removes hot-loop conversion overhead by using existing scaled int16 lineitem discount/quantity versions and pre-scaling partsupp supplycost once per filtered key, while retaining dense year lookup and direct-array aggregation.
